maml:description.maml:para: Remove one or all items from the data cache.
Removes a given item from the in-memory data cache, either by its name or ID, or clears the entire cache.
Your API endpoint ID to remove from the data cache.
Your API endpoint name to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) configuration ID to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) policy ID to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) policy name to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) configuration name to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) policy ID to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) policy name to remove from the data cache.
Your client list ID to remove from the data cache.
Your client list name to remove from the data cache.
Your EdgeWorker ID to remove from the data cache.
Your EdgeWorker name to remove from the data cache.
Your property Include's ID to remove from the data cache.
Your property Include's name to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Edge Trust Store CA Set ID to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Edge Trust Store CA Set name to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Origin Keystore client certificate ID to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Origin Keystore client certificate name to remove from the data cache.
Your property's ID to remove from the data cache.
Your Property's name to remove from the data cache.
Your API endpoint ID to remove from the data cache.
Your API endpoint name to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) configuration ID to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) configuration name to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) policy ID to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) policy name to remove from the data cache.
Your client list ID to remove from the data cache.
Your client list name to remove from the data cache.
Your EdgeWorker ID to remove from the data cache.
Your EdgeWorker name to remove from the data cache.
Your property Include's ID to remove from the data cache.
Your property Include's name to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Edge Trust Store CA Set ID to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Edge Trust Store CA Set name to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Origin Keystore client certificate ID to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Origin Keystore client certificate name to remove from the data cache.
Your property's ID to remove from the data cache.
Your Property's name to remove from the data cache.
Reset Akamai PowerShell options.
Removes the Akamai PowerShell options JSON file located at either `~/.akamai-pwsh/options.json` or the location found in the `AkamaiOptionsPath` environment variable.
Clear EdgeGrid credentials environment variables.

Removes any environment variables relating to your EdgeGrid credentials. Specifically, if you do not specify a `-Section` parameters the following variables are removed: `AKAMAI_HOST`, `AKAMAI_CLIENT_TOKEN`, `AKAMAI_ACCESS_TOKEN`, `AKAMAI_CLIENT_SECRET`, and `AKAMAI_ACCOUNT_KEY` (if it exists). If you specify a `-Section`, that value is used as a prefix between `AKAMAI` and the remainder of the variable name, for example, `-Section MyCreds` clears `AKAMAI_MYCREDS_HOST` and so on.
Clear Netstorage credentials environment variables.

Removes any environment variables relating to your Netstorage credentials. Specifically, if you do not specify a `-Section` parameter the following variables are removed: `NETSTORAGE_KEY`, `NETSTORAGE_GROUP`, `NETSTORAGE_HOST`, `NETSTORAGE_ID`, and `NETSTORAGE_CPCODE`. If you specify a `-Section`, that value is used as a prefix between `NETSTORAGE` and the remainder of the variable name, for example, `-Section MyCreds` clears `NETSTORAGE_MYCREDS_KEY` and so on.
Export EdgeGrid credentials to a file.
Takes a given set of EdgeGrid credentials and writes them to an `.edgerc` file, either in the `[default]` section or a custom section if provided.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The API credential's hostname.
The API credential's client token.
Access token.
The API credential's client secret.
Overwrite an existing section in your EdgeRCFile, if it exists.
Access token.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The API credential's client secret.
The API credential's client token.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Overwrite an existing section in your EdgeRCFile, if it exists.
The API credential's hostname.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Export Netstorage credentials to a file.
Takes a given set of Netstorage credentials and writes them to an `.nsrc` file, either in the `[default]` section or a custom section if provided.
NSRC file to update/create.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
HTTP API key of your Netstorage upload account.
The `id` element to include in the edge auth token.
Domain prefix of your Netstorage group.
The API credential's hostname.
Content provider code from your Netstorage group.
Overwrite an existing section in your EdgeRCFile, if it exists.
Content provider code from your Netstorage group.
Overwrite an existing section in your EdgeRCFile, if it exists.
Domain prefix of your Netstorage group.
The API credential's hostname.
The `id` element to include in the edge auth token.
HTTP API key of your Netstorage upload account.
NSRC file to update/create.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Get Akamai Options.
Returns a global `AkamaiOptions` variable. If the variable doesn't exist, the function creates it.
Get API client authorization grants.
Returns information about the Akamai API client, including the APIs that the client is authorized to access.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Output API response body as a PSCustomObject.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Output API response body as a PSCustomObject.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Retrieve EdgeGrid credentials.

Loads EdgeGrid credentials by checking for environment variables (`AKAMAI_HOST`, `AKAMAI_CLIENT_TOKEN`, and other) or your `.edgerc` file. These can then be used to sign requests, or be saved to a file with `Export-EdgeGridCredentials`. If you specify an `.edgerc` file, that will be tried directly. If not, it checks environment variables first, then the default `.edgerc` file location (`~/.edgerc`), finally throwing an error if no credentials can be found.

If you specify a `-Section` parameter, this checks for environment variables with that value as a prefix, for example, `-Section MyCreds` instructs the function to look for `AKAMAI_MYCREDS_HOST` and so on.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Get NetStorage credentials.
Gets NetStorage authentication credentials from environment variables or a `.nsrc` file.
NSRC file to update/create.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
NSRC file to update/create.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Load EdgeGrid credentials to environment variables.

Takes in either a set of EdgeGrid credentials or an `.edgerc` file and section and sets the appropriate environment variables for subsequent requests. This allows you to set the credentials your requests will use for a given session in your shell, and avoid having to provide these for every command. To clear your credentials use `Clear-EdgeGridCredentials`.

Alongside your credentials you can also specify an `EnvironmentPrefix`. This will be added as a prefix to your environment variables, for example, `-EnvironmentPrefix MyCreds` results in `AKAMAI_MYCREDS_HOST`, and so on. These credentials are loaded by any command which specifies `-Section` of `MyCreds`.
Access token.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The API credential's client secret.
The API credential's client token.
String to prepend environment variables, for example, with a value of `MyCreds` the function creates environment variables `AKAMAI_MYCREDS_HOST`.
The API credential's hostname.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use `Get-AccountSwitchKey` (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
String to prepend environment variables, for example, with a value of `MyCreds` the function creates environment variables `AKAMAI_MYCREDS_HOST` and so on.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Access token.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The API credential's client secret.
The API credential's client token.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
String to prepend environment variables, for example, with a value of `MyCreds` the function will create environment variables `AKAMAI_MYCREDS_HOST` and so on.
The API credential's hostname.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Load Netstorage usage API credentials to environment variables.

Takes in either a set of Netstorage credentials or a .nsrc file and section and sets the appropriate environment variables for subsequent request. This allows you to set the credentials your requests will use for a given session in your shell, and avoid having to provide these for every command. To clear your credentials use Clear-NetstorageCredentials.

Alongside your credentials you can also specify an EnvironmentPrefix. This will be added as a prefix to your environment variables, for example, `-EnvironmentPrefix MyCreds` will result in NETSTORAGE_MYCREDS_CPCODE, and so on. These credentials will be loaded by any command which specifies -Section of MyCreds.
Content provider code from your Netstorage group.
String to prepend environment variables, for example, with a value of MyCreds the function will create environment variables AKAMAI_MYCREDS_HOST and so on.
Domain prefix of your Netstorage group.
The API credential's hostname.
The `id` element to include in the edge auth token.
HTTP API key of your Netstorage upload account.
String to prepend environment variables, for example, with a value of MyCreds the function will create environment variables AKAMAI_MYCREDS_HOST and so on.
NSRC file to update/create.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Content provider code from your Netstorage group.
String to prepend environment variables, for example, with a value of MyCreds the function will create environment variables AKAMAI_MYCREDS_HOST and so on.
Domain prefix of your Netstorage group.
The API credential's hostname.
The `id` element to include in the edge auth token.
HTTP API key of your Netstorage upload account.
NSRC file to update/create.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Send NetStorage authorization wrapper.

Provides an authorization wrapper around `Invoke-RestMethod` for use with the NetStorage API. > Note: This function has been superceded by `Invoke-NetstorageRequest`, and will not receive further updates.
Your request path.
The API endpoint's action type.
A hashtable of additional options for a request.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string.
File to send with upload HTTP requests.
The file to write response data to.
Your NetStorage auth file (NSRC) to read credentials from.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The API endpoint's action type.
A hashtable of additional options for a request.
Your NetStorage auth file (NSRC) to read credentials from.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string.
File to send with upload HTTP requests.
The file to write response data to.
Your request path.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Send an authorized HTTPS request to an Akamai RESTful web service.

Constructs a call to `Invoke-WebRequest` from the given `-Path`, `-Method`, and other additional parameters and adds necessary authorization details retrieved from `Get-AkamaiCredentials`. The response is formatted into a `PSCustomObject` including `Status`, a hashtable of headers, and a `PSCustomObject` of the response body. Error handling is configured based on the current `AkamaiOptions` variable.
Your request path.
Request method.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The query parameters in a hashtable.
An additional headers in a hashtable.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string.
File to send with upload HTTP requests.
The file to write response data to.
Maximum body size with which to calculate the EdgeGrid signature. Defaults to 128kb, and should not be changed unless your API client has a lower value.
The retry index. Used to determine whether a failed request should be retried.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Do not validate request headers meet expected format.

When the switch parameter is present, this ignores HTTP error statuses and continues to process responses. The error responses are written to the pipeline just as if they were successful. > Note: This parameter is only applicable with PowerShell 7+.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
An additional headers in a hashtable.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
File to send with upload HTTP requests.
Maximum body size with which to calculate the EdgeGrid signature. Defaults to 128kb, and should not be changed unless your API client has a lower value.
Request method.
The file to write response data to.
Your request path.
The query parameters in a hashtable.
The retry index. Used to determine whether a failed request should be retried.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Do not validate request headers meet expected format.

When the switch parameter is present, this ignores HTTP error statuses and continues to process responses. The error responses are written to the pipeline just as if they were successful. > Note: This parameter is only applicable with PowerShell 7+.
Send an authorized HTTPS request to an Akamai RESTful web service.

Constructs a call to `Invoke-RestMethod` from the given `-Path`, `-Method`, and other additional parameters and adds necessary authorization details retrieved from `Get-AkamaiCredentials`. The response is formatted into a `PSCustomObject` of the response body. > Note: This function has been superceded by `Invoke-AkamaiRequest` and will not receive further updates.
Request method.
Your request path.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The query parameters in a hashtable.
An additional headers in a hashtable.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string.
File to send with upload HTTP requests.
The file to write response data to.
Maximum body size with which to calculate the EdgeGrid signature. Defaults to 128kb, and should not be changed unless your API client has a lower value.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
When the switch parameter is present, this includes the response header data in the output.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
An additional headers in a hashtable.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When the switch parameter is present, this includes the response header data in the output.
File to send with upload HTTP requests.
Maximum body size with which to calculate the EdgeGrid signature. Defaults to 128kb, and should not be changed unless your API client has a lower value.
Request method.
The file to write response data to.
Your request path.
The query parameters in a hashtable.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Make a request to the Netstorage usage API.

This function is an upgrade of the older `Invoke-NSAPIRequest`, which interfaces between Netstorage sub-module functions and the Netstorage usage API. Credentials are loaded from environment variables or a provided `.nsrc` file and requests are signed. This function uses `Invoke-RestMethod` at its core.
Your request path.
The API endpoint's action type.
A hashtable of additional options for a request.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string.
File to send with upload HTTP requests.
The file to write response data to.
The retry index. Used to determine whether a failed request should be retried.
NSRC file to update/create.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The API endpoint's action type.
A hashtable of additional options for a request.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string.
File to send with upload HTTP requests.
NSRC file to update/create.
The file to write response data to.
Your request path.
The retry index. Used to determine whether a failed request should be retried.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Create a default data cache object.
Creates a `PSCustomObject` of a given format and assigns it to a globally-scoped variable. This variable holds the in-memory data cache if this feature is enabled.
Populate Akamai options.

Creates a new `PSCustomObject` of the supported options and writes that object to a disk, either at the default location of `~/.akamai-pwsh/options.json` or to a location specified in the `AkamaiOptionsPath` environment variable.
Create an Edge auth token.

Various Akamai services make use of Token Authentication or similar services, which require encrypted tokens. This function will create such a token from the given inputs for you to use in testing or live requests.
An `acl` element to include in the edge auth token.
An algorithm to use in calculating the edge auth token signature. Defaults to `sha256` but also accepts `sha1` and `md5`.
The `data` element to include in the edge auth token.
the field delimiter. Defaults to `~`.
The token duration in hours. During token generation, it's converted to seconds.
The token duration in minutes. During token generation, it's converted to seconds.
The token duration in seconds.
The token end time in epoch format.
When the switch parameter is present, it forces a URL token element to be URL-encoded.
The `id` element to include in the edge auth token.
The `ip` element to include in the edge auth token.
The `salt` element to include in the edge auth token.
Encryption key.
The token start time in epoch format.
An algorithm to use in calculating the edge auth token signature. Defaults to `sha256` but also accepts `sha1` and `md5`.
The `data` element to include in the edge auth token.
the field delimiter. Defaults to `~`.
The token duration in hours. During token generation, it's converted to seconds.
The token duration in minutes. During token generation, it's converted to seconds.
The token duration in seconds.
The token end time in epoch format.
When the switch parameter is present, it forces a URL token element to be URL-encoded.
The `id` element to include in the edge auth token.
The `ip` element to include in the edge auth token.
The `salt` element to include in the edge auth token.
Encryption key.
The token start time in epoch format.
The `url` element to include in the edge auth token.
An `acl` element to include in the edge auth token.
An algorithm to use in calculating the edge auth token signature. Defaults to `sha256` but also accepts `sha1` and `md5`.
The `data` element to include in the edge auth token.
the field delimiter. Defaults to `~`.
The token duration in hours. During token generation, it's converted to seconds.
The token duration in minutes. During token generation, it's converted to seconds.
The token duration in seconds.
The token end time in epoch format.
When the switch parameter is present, it forces a URL token element to be URL-encoded.
The `id` element to include in the edge auth token.
The `ip` element to include in the edge auth token.
The `salt` element to include in the edge auth token.
Encryption key.
The token start time in epoch format.
The `url` element to include in the edge auth token.
Add to or update the Data Cache.

Creates or updates elements in the in-memory Akamai data cache, such as properties, AppSec configurations, and more, and is called by various sub-module functions automatically when elements included in the cache scope are retrieved from the Akamai APIs.
Your API endpoint ID to remove from the data cache.
Your API endpoint name to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) configuration ID to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) configuration name to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) configuration name to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) policy ID to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) policy name to remove from the data cache.
Your client list ID to remove from the data cache.
Your client list name to remove from the data cache.
Your EdgeWorker ID to remove from the data cache.
Your EdgeWorker name to remove from the data cache.
Your property Include's ID to remove from the data cache.
Your property Include's name to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Edge Trust Store CA Set ID to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Edge Trust Store CA Set name to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Origin Keystore client certificate ID to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Origin Keystore client certificate name to remove from the data cache.
Your property's ID to remove from the data cache.
Your Property's name to remove from the data cache.
Your API endpoint ID to remove from the data cache.
Your API endpoint name to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) configuration ID to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) configuration name to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) policy ID to remove from the data cache.
Your AppSec (AAP/Kona) policy name to remove from the data cache.
Your client list ID to remove from the data cache.
Your client list name to remove from the data cache.
Your EdgeWorker ID to remove from the data cache.
Your EdgeWorker name to remove from the data cache.
Your property Include's ID to remove from the data cache.
Your property Include's name to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Edge Trust Store CA Set ID to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Edge Trust Store CA Set name to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Origin Keystore client certificate ID to remove from the data cache.
Your MTLS Origin Keystore client certificate name to remove from the data cache.
Your property's ID to remove from the data cache.
Your Property's name to remove from the data cache.
Update Akamai Options.

Updates any of the supported Akamai options, either as booleans to enable or disable certain features, for example, `-EnableErrorRetries $true`, or as integers to set various thresholds and limits, for example, `-MaxErrorRetries 2`. You can also reset all options to their defaults with `-RestoreDefaults`. > Note: You can't set any options and restore the others to default in a single command. You need to restore the defaults first, then set whatever options you wish.
Forces the addition of a header disabling prefixes in all Property API calls, regardless of API client setting.
Enables the in-memory data cache feature, which caches resolution of names to IDs and speeds up subsequent API calls.
Enables retries for applicable requests when the API endpoint responds with a 5XX status code.
Enables retries for applicable requests when the API endpoint responds with a 429 status code.

Enables outputting a warning when you have reached a set percentage of your rate limit, for example, if the limit is 500 requests, your warning percentage is 80 and you have expended 400 requests, any further requests before the rate limit repleneshes will result in a warning being printed to the shell.

Enables the recommended actions provider, which will suggest actions in the event of certain errors. This feature requires the use of the `PSFeedbackProvider` Powershell experimental feature, and currently has an extremely limited scope.
Set the initial wait time (in seconds) after which PowerShell will retry an applicable request.
Set the maximum number of retries which will be attempted for applicable requests in the event of an error.
Set the percentage used of the rate limit for a given endpoint, above which PowerShell will warn you of rate limit exhaustion.
Restore Akamai options to the default values.
Forces the addition of a header disabling prefixes in all Property API calls, regardless of API client setting.
Enables the in-memory data cache feature, which caches resolution of names to IDs and speeds up subsequent API calls.
Enables retries for applicable requests when the API endpoint responds with a 5XX status code.
Enables retries for applicable requests when the API endpoint responds with a 429 status code.

Enables outputting a warning when you have reached a set percentage of your rate limit, for example, if the limit is 500 requests, your warning percentage is 80 and you have expended 400 requests, any further requests before the rate limit repleneshes will result in a warning being printed to the shell.

Enables the recommended actions provider, which will suggest actions in the event of certain errors. This feature requires the use of the `PSFeedbackProvider` Powershell experimental feature, and currently has an extremely limited scope.
Set the initial wait time (in seconds) after which PowerShell will retry an applicable request.
Set the maximum number of retries which will be attempted for applicable requests in the event of an error.
Set the percentage used of the rate limit for a given endpoint, above which PowerShell will warn you of rate limit exhaustion.
Restore Akamai options to the default values.
Test your EdgeGrid credentials.
Validates your EdgeGrid credentials match the expected format by regular expression. No API requests are made to perform the validation, so your credentials may still not function properly.
The API credential's hostname.
The API credential's client token.
Access token.
The API credential's client secret.
Access token.
The API credential's client secret.
The API credential's client token.
The API credential's hostname.
Test an Akamai endpoint.
This function is a simple wrapper to `Invoke-AkamaiRestMethod` with fewer options. It provides a more simplified way of testing API endpoints.
Your request path.
Request method.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string.
The `Accept` request header value.
The `Content-Type` request header value.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The `Accept` request header value.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string.
The `Content-Type` request header value.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Request method.
Your request path.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
